description: FrankenLibC CVE Arena — Normalized Corpus Index (bd-1m5.5)

expected_stock_schema.signal: string|null — signal name if crashes (SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, etc.)
expected_stock_schema.exit_code: integer|null — expected exit code if no crash
expected_stock_schema.description: string — human-readable description of stock behavior

expected_tsm_schema.crashes: boolean — whether FrankenLibC TSM prevents the crash
expected_tsm_schema.exit_code: integer — expected exit code under TSM
expected_tsm_schema.healing_actions: string[] — TSM healing actions applied (ClampSize, TruncateWithNull, etc.)
expected_tsm_schema.description: string — human-readable description of TSM behavior

scenarios.description: __vsyslog_internal heap overflow via crafted ident/format string causing integer overflow in buffer size calculation

expected_stock.description: Heap corruption from integer overflow in buffer size calculation; controlled heap overwrite enables local privilege escalation

expected_tsm.description: TSM detects overflow via trailing canary and clamps the buffer size; truncation prevents the overwrite

scenarios.description: iconv() writes up to 8 bytes past the output buffer when converting to ISO-2022-CN-EXT due to incorrect bounds computation in the internal encoder

expected_stock.description: Heap corruption from 8-byte overwrite past output buffer; may crash at free() or silently corrupt adjacent allocations enabling code execution

expected_tsm.description: Trailing canary detects overflow at free(); ClampSize would prevent the overwrite in iconv internal memcpy; bounds computation catches the out-of-range write

scenarios.description: nscd netgroup cache corruption via stack overflow and null pointer dereference; multiple memory safety violations in netgroup response handling

expected_stock.description: Stack overflow from recursive netgroup expansion; null pointer dereference in cache lookup; heap corruption from incorrect size calculations

expected_tsm.description: TSM detects stack overflow via canary, clamps recursive depth, returns safe default for missing cache entries, and quarantines corrupted allocations

scenarios.description: regcomp() double-frees internal regex parse tree nodes when malloc fails during compilation of a complex regex, due to incorrect error-path cleanup

expected_stock.description: Double free detected by glibc allocator; aborts with 'double free or corruption' message

expected_tsm.description: Generational arena detects the double-free via generation counter mismatch; IgnoreDoubleFree healing action silently discards the second free; quarantine prevents the freed memory from being reused during the vulnerable window
